message.content:
Add new rubric/trigger-migration linked terms to RBS0-SpecTop.adoc at `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`.

This is a large file. Read it in chunks as needed. DO NOT modify existing content — only add new entries.

## Part 1: Mapping Section Additions

The mapping section starts at line 8 with `// tag::mapping-section[]`. Add these new entries in the appropriate locations:

### New rbtgr_* family (after the rbtga_* block around line 75, before the rbtgo_* block)

Add a comment line and the new entries:
```
// Recipe Bottle Term Google Rubric (per-vessel build definition)
:rbtgr_rubric:              <<rbtgr_rubric,RB Rubric>>
:rbtgr_rubric_s:            <<rbtgr_rubric,RB Rubrics>>
:rbtgr_build_yaml:          <<rbtgr_build_yaml,Rubric YAML>>
:rbtgr_vessel_trigger:      <<rbtgr_vessel_trigger,Vessel Trigger>>
:rbtgr_vessel_repo:         <<rbtgr_vessel_repo,Vessel Repo>>
:rbtgr_provenance:          <<rbtgr_provenance,Rubric Provenance>>
```

### New rbtgi_* entries (add to existing rbtgi block, after rbtgi_rbra_file_s around line 68)

```
:rbtgi_source_connection:   <<rbtgi_source_connection,Source Connection>>
:rbtgi_source_link:         <<rbtgi_source_link,Source Link>>
```

### New rbtgo_* entries (add to existing rbtgo block, after rbtgo_trigger_build around line 97)

```
:rbtgo_gdc_establish:            <<rbtgo_gdc_establish,rbgm_gdc_establish>>
:rbtgo_rubric_inscribe:          <<rbtgo_rubric_inscribe,rbf_inscribe>>
```

### Update existing rbtgo_trigger_build (line 97)

Change:
```
:rbtgo_trigger_build:             <<rbtgo_trigger_build,rbf_build>>
```
To:
```
:rbtgo_trigger_build:             <<rbtgo_trigger_build,rbf_trigger>>
```

### New rbrr_* entries for Developer Connect (add after existing rbrr block, around line 268)

Add a comment and entries:
```
// Recipe Bottle Regime: Developer Connect
:rbrr_group_developer_connect:  <<rbrr_group_developer_connect,Developer Connect Group>>
:rbrr_gdc_connection_name:      <<rbrr_gdc_connection_name,RBRR_GDC_CONNECTION_NAME>>
:rbrr_gdc_region:               <<rbrr_gdc_region,RBRR_GDC_REGION>>
```

Note: No rbrr_gdc_repo_link — repo links are per-vessel, not regime config.

## Part 2: Definition Additions

### Rubric definitions (add a new section "=== Rubric Definitions" after the Ark Definitions section, which ends around line 1165)

Add BEFORE the "=== Support Infrastructure Definitions" heading:

```
=== Rubric Definitions

[[rbtgr_rubric]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_build_config axig_build_trigger⟧
{rbtgr_rubric}::
The composite per-vessel build definition comprising a committed
{rbtgr_build_yaml},
a
{rbtgr_vessel_trigger},
and a
{rbtgr_vessel_repo}.
Created and maintained by
{rbtgo_rubric_inscribe}.

[[rbtgr_build_yaml]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_build_config⟧
{rbtgr_build_yaml}::
The committed `cloudbuild.yaml` file for a vessel, generated from step scripts and regime pins by the modified stitch function.
All values baked at inscribe time — zero custom substitution overrides at dispatch.

[[rbtgr_vessel_trigger]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_build_trigger⟧
{rbtgr_vessel_trigger}::
The per-vessel Cloud Build trigger resource in GCP.
Dispatches builds via `triggers.run` when invoked by
{rbtgo_trigger_build}.

[[rbtgr_vessel_repo]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_repo_link⟧
{rbtgr_vessel_repo}::
The per-vessel mini-repo on GitHub containing only the build material (cloudbuild.yaml, Dockerfile, build context).
Cloud Build sees only this repo via Developer Connect — the main repo is never exposed.
Managed by
{rbtgo_rubric_inscribe}.

[[rbtgr_provenance]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_slsa_provenance⟧
{rbtgr_provenance}::
SLSA v1.0 build provenance attestation auto-generated by Google for trigger-invoked builds.
Records build inputs, environment, and outputs as a cryptographically signed attestation on the built image.
```

### Developer Connect definitions (add after Rubric Definitions, still before Support Infrastructure)

```
=== Developer Connect Definitions

[[rbtgi_source_connection]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_developer_connect⟧
{rbtgi_source_connection}::
The Developer Connect connection providing an OAuth-authenticated bridge from GCP to a GitHub organization.
One per project. Established manually via
{rbtgo_gdc_establish}.

[[rbtgi_source_link]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_repo_link⟧
{rbtgi_source_link}::
A git repository link within the
{rbtgi_source_connection},
binding it to a specific
{rbtgr_vessel_repo}.
Per-vessel, created programmatically by
{rbtgo_rubric_inscribe}.
```

### New operation sections

Find the existing `[[rbtgo_trigger_build]]` section (around line 737). ADD before it (between the `== {rbtr_director} Operations` heading and the trigger_build section):

```
[[rbtgo_gdc_establish]]
// ⟦axl_voices axo_guide axe_human_guide⟧
=== {rbtgo_gdc_establish}

Establishes the
{rbtgi_source_connection}
by walking the operator through the Developer Connect OAuth browser authorization flow.

include::RBSGD-gdc_establish.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_rubric_inscribe]]
// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧
=== {rbtgo_rubric_inscribe}

The single rubric lifecycle command: checks pin freshness, generates
{rbtgr_build_yaml}
from step scripts and regime pins, verifies committed in main repo, syncs to
{rbtgr_vessel_repo},
and creates
{rbtgr_vessel_trigger}
if it does not exist.

include::RBSRI-rubric_inscribe.adoc[]

```

### Update the trigger_build description (around line 741)

The current line after `=== {rbtgo_trigger_build}` says:
```
Builds container image from vessel specification using Cloud Build and stores in repository.
```

Replace with:
```
Dispatches a build via `triggers.run` using the vessel's
{rbtgr_vessel_trigger}.
All values baked in
{rbtgr_build_yaml}
— zero custom substitution overrides.
```

### Regime variable definitions for Developer Connect

Find the regime variable definitions section. Look for where rbrr_* variables are defined (search for `[[rbrr_depot_project_id]]` or similar patterns). There should be a regime variables section. Add a new group after the existing groups:

Search for existing regime definition patterns - they may be in an included file (RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc). If so, note this for later and skip this step — the subdoc update will handle it.

## Important constraints

- Read the file in chunks before editing — it's very large
- Only add new content, do not modify existing content (except the two specific updates noted)
- Maintain exact MCM formatting patterns
- Use the exact voicing annotation format: `// ⟦axl_voices MOTIF⟧`
- Blank line between each definition
- No trailing whitespace

   424→// TIER 1: WHY AND WHAT

   426→
   427→== System Overview
   428→
   429→=== Core Trust Challenge
   430→
   431→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   432→{st_container}-based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   433→
   434→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   435→
   436→*
   437→{st_container_s}
   438→provide powerful tool isolation
   439→* But they need controlled access to do useful work
   440→* Standard
   441→{st_container}
   442→networking grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   443→
   444→=== The Bottle Pattern
   445→
   446→{at_rbm_system}
   447→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   448→{st_container}
   449→type here referred to as a
   450→{at_bottle_container}:
   451→
   452→. File Access Boundary
   453→*
   454→{at_bottle_container}
   455→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   456→{st_volume_mount_s}
   457→* No other access to
   458→{at_workstation}
   459→filesystem
   460→
   461→. Network Boundary
   462→*
   463→{at_bottle_container}
   464→has no direct network access
   465→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   466→{at_sentry_container},
   467→a
   468→{st_container}
   469→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   470→* Only declared
   471→{st_port_map_s}
   472→and destinations allowed
   473→* Full logging of allowed and denied traffic
   474→
   475→Each
   476→{at_bottle_service}
   477→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   478→
   479→* One
   480→{at_sentry_container}
   481→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   482→* One
   483→{at_censer_container}
   484→establishing and configuring the network namespace
   485→* One
   486→{at_bottle_container}
   487→running the actual service, sharing the censer's network namespace
   488→* Clear configuration in a single
   489→{at_nameplate}
   490→file
   491→
   492→The
   493→{at_censer_container}
   494→is a privileged container that establishes the network namespace, configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
   495→{at_sentry_container},
   496→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
   497→{at_bottle_container}
   498→via `--net=container:censer`.
   499→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
   500→{at_bottle_container}
   501→experiences only a functional path to its
   502→{at_sentry_container}
   503→gateway
   504→
   505→=== Security Properties
   506→
   507→The
   508→{at_rbm_system}
   509→maintains following essential guarantees:
   510→
   511→. File Access Control
   512→*
   513→{at_bottle_container_s}
   514→can access only explicitly configured
   515→{st_volume_mount_s}
   516→* No access to broader system resources
   517→
   518→. Network Isolation

   902→[[mkc_interface_check]]
   903→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_scripted⟧
   904→==== {mkc_interface_check}
   905→
   906→A utility that verifies network interface presence and configuration within a container context:
   907→
   908→[source,makefile]
   909→----
   910→# Waits until interface has an IPv4 address assigned, fails after timeout
   911→# @param 1 interface   Network interface name to check
   912→# @param 2 timeout_s   Number of seconds to wait before failing
   913→define check_interface
   914→timeout $(2)s sh -c "while ! ip addr show $(1) | grep -q \"inet \"; do sleep 0.2; done"
   915→endef
   916→----
   917→
   918→This utility generates precise validation commands for network interface readiness probes, accepting an interface name and timeout duration as parameters.

  1118→=== Ark Definitions
  1119→
  1120→[[rbtga_ark]]
  1121→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_group⟧
  1122→{rbtga_ark}::
  1123→The coherent unit of a
  1124→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1125→build output: a paired set of artifacts in the
  1126→{rbtgi_repository}
  1127→sharing a common
  1128→{rbtga_consecration}
  1129→but distinguished by suffix.
  1130→An ark comprises the
  1131→{rbtga_ark_image}
  1132→(`-image` suffix) containing the container image and the
  1133→{rbtga_ark_about}
  1134→(`-about` suffix) containing build provenance and metadata.
  1135→Arks are conjured atomically by
  1136→{rbtgo_ark_conjure}

  1143→The timestamp-based identifier marking when a vessel was consecrated into an
  1144→{rbtga_ark}.
  1145→Format: `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ` or similar timestamp pattern.
  1146→Combined with vessel to form complete artifact tags: `«VESSEL»:«CONSECRATION»-image` and `«VESSEL»:«CONSECRATION»-about`.
  1147→
  1148→[[rbtga_ark_image]]
  1149→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧
  1150→{rbtga_ark_image}::
  1151→The container image artifact within an

  1157→[[rbtga_ark_about]]
  1158→// ⟦axl_voices axig_generic_artifact⟧
  1159→{rbtga_ark_about}::
  1160→The metadata artifact within an

  1380→=== Local Architecture Definitions
  1381→
  1382→[[at_sessile_service]]
  1383→{at_sessile_service}::
  1384→A long-running
  1385→{at_bottle_service}
  1386→that starts via
  1387→{opbs_bottle_start}
  1388→and persists until explicitly stopped.
  1389→The sessile pattern is used for services that maintain state and provide ongoing functionality.
  1390→
  1391→[[at_agile_service]]
  1392→{at_agile_service}::
  1393→A transient
  1394→{at_bottle_service}
  1395→that executes via
  1396→{opbr_bottle_run}
  1397→and terminates when its command completes.
  1398→The agile pattern is used for one-shot operations that produce output and exit.
  1399→

1399→
  1400→[[at_bottle_service]]
  1401→{at_bottle_service}::
  1402→A complete service instance consisting of one
  1403→{at_sentry_container},
  1404→one
  1405→{at_censer_container},
  1406→and one
  1407→{at_bottle_container}
  1408→working together to provide secure functionality.
  1409→Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
  1410→
  1411→[[at_sentry_container]]
  1412→{at_sentry_container}::
  1413→A privileged container that enforces network security policies for a
  1414→{at_bottle_service}.
  1415→It connects to both
  1416→{at_transit_network}
  1417→and
  1418→{at_enclave_network},
  1419→controlling all network traffic and providing DNS services.
  1420→
  1421→[[at_censer_container]]
  1422→{at_censer_container}::
  1423→A privileged container using a minimal image that establishes and configures the network namespace before the
  1424→{at_bottle_container}
  1425→starts.
  1426→It sets up routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
  1427→{at_sentry_container},
  1428→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
  1429→{at_bottle_container}
  1430→via `--net=container:censer`.
  1431→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet.
  1432→
  1433→[[at_bottle_container]]
  1434→{at_bottle_container}::
  1435→A container that runs the actual service functionality, inheriting its network namespace from the
  1436→{at_censer_container}.
  1437→It is connected only to the
  1438→{at_enclave_network}
  1439→and configured with specific
  1440→{st_volume_mount_s}.
  1441→All external communication flows through its associated
  1442→{at_sentry_container}.
  1443→
  1444→[[at_enclave_network]]
  1445→{at_enclave_network}::
  1446→An isolated network connecting a
  1447→{at_bottle_container}
  1448→to its
  1449→{at_sentry_container},
  1450→using
  1451→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
  1452→for addressing.
  1453→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only network communication path for the
  1454→{at_bottle_container}.
  1455→
  1456→[[at_transit_network]]
  1457→{at_transit_network}::
  1458→The network interface that connects a
  1459→{at_sentry_container}
  1460→to the
  1461→{at_workstation_p}
  1462→network resources.
  1463→This network enables controlled external communication while maintaining security boundaries.
  1464→
  1465→[[at_nameplate]]
  1466→{at_nameplate}::
  1467→A configuration file that defines all aspects of a
  1468→{at_bottle_service},
  1469→including its images, network settings, and security policies.
  1470→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the
  1471→{at_rbm_system}
  1472→can instantiate.
  1473→
  1474→[[at_ebpf_program]]
  1475→{at_ebpf_program}::
  1476→An eBPF TC (Traffic Control) filter program attached to the
  1477→{at_bottle_container_p}
  1478→veth interface that intercepts and rewrites Ethernet frames at the packet level.
  1479→This program ensures all
  1480→{at_bottle_container}
  1481→network traffic routes through its associated
  1482→{at_sentry_container}
  1483→without requiring network namespace manipulation or special container privileges.
  1484→
  1485→[[st_container]]
  1486→{st_container}::
  1487→A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.
  1488→
  1489→[[st_volume_mount]]
  1490→{st_volume_mount}::
  1491→A configuration that allows
  1492→{st_container_s}
  1493→to access specified portions of the host filesystem.
  1494→
  1495→[[st_port_map]]
  1496→{st_port_map}::
  1497→A specification of how network ports should be exposed and mapped between
  1498→{st_container_s}
  1499→and the host system.
  1500→
  1501→[[at_sentry_image]]
  1502→{at_sentry_image}::
  1503→A
  1504→{st_image}
  1505→that contains the security enforcement functionality for a
  1506→{at_sentry_container}.
  1507→
  1508→[[at_moniker]]
  1509→{at_moniker}::
  1510→A unique identifier that distinguishes each
  1511→{at_bottle_service}
  1512→instance within the
  1513→{at_rbm_system}.
  1514→This identifier forms the basis for container names and network identifiers.
  1515→
  1516→[[at_rbm_console]]
  1517→{at_rbm_console}::
  1518→The primary operational automation that implements the
  1519→{at_rbm_system}
  1520→lifecycle rules for managing
  1521→{at_bottle_service_s}.
  1522→
  1523→[[at_rbm_config]]
  1524→{at_rbm_config}::
  1525→Configuration settings for the
  1526→{at_rbm_system},
  1527→including
  1528→{at_rbm_secret_s}
  1529→and environment-specific parameters.
  1530→This file typically remains outside version control to protect sensitive data.
  1531→
  1532→[[at_rbm_secret]]
  1533→{at_rbm_secret}::
  1534→A sensitive configuration value needed by the
  1535→{at_rbm_system},
  1536→such as registry credentials.
  1537→Kept separate from version control.
  1538→
  1539→[[at_rbm_repo]]
  1540→{at_rbm_repo}::
  1541→The version control repository containing the
  1542→{at_rbm_system}
  1543→implementation, including operational automation,
  1544→{at_nameplate_s},
  1545→and documentation.
  1546→
  1547→[[at_user_repo]]
  1548→{at_user_repo}::

1549→A version control repository owned by an
  1550→{at_operator}
  1551→where the
  1552→{at_rbm_system}
  1553→is instantiated.
  1554→The
  1555→{at_operator}
  1556→configures it by setting up regime files and creating
  1557→{at_nameplate_s}
  1558→to define
  1559→{at_bottle_service_s}.
  1560→
  1561→[[at_container_registry]]
  1562→{at_container_registry}::
  1563→A service that stores and provides access to
  1564→{st_published_image_s}.
  1565→
  1566→[[at_startup_script]]
  1567→{at_startup_script}::
  1568→A shell script that performs initialization and configuration tasks when starting a
  1569→{at_bottle_service}.
  1570→
  1571→[[at_transit_network_name]]
  1572→{at_transit_network_name}::
  1573→The unique network name formed by appending `-uplink` to the service's
  1574→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1575→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-uplink`.
  1576→
  1577→[[at_enclave_network_name]]
  1578→{at_enclave_network_name}::
  1579→The unique network name formed by appending `-enclave` to the service's
  1580→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1581→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-enclave`.
  1582→
  1583→[[at_enclave_namespace_name]]
  1584→{at_enclave_namespace_name}::
  1585→The unique namespace name formed by appending `-namespace` to the service's
  1586→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1587→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-namespace`.
  1588→
  1589→[[at_bottle_container_name]]
  1590→{at_bottle_container_name}::
  1591→The unique container name formed by appending `-bottle` to the service's
  1592→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1593→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle`.
  1594→
  1595→[[at_sentry_container_name]]
  1596→{at_sentry_container_name}::
  1597→The unique container name formed by appending `-sentry` to the service's
  1598→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1599→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry`.

=== Rubric Definitions

[[rbtgr_rubric]]

The composite per-vessel build definition comprising a committed
{rbtgr_build_yaml},
a
{rbtgr_vessel_trigger},
and a
{rbtgr_vessel_repo}.
Created and maintained by
{rbtgo_rubric_inscribe}.

[[rbtgr_build_yaml]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_build_config⟧
{rbtgr_build_yaml}::
The committed `cloudbuild.yaml` file for a vessel, generated from step scripts and regime pins by the modified stitch function.
All values baked at inscribe time — zero custom substitution overrides at dispatch.

[[rbtgr_vessel_trigger]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_build_trigger⟧
{rbtgr_vessel_trigger}::
The per-vessel Cloud Build trigger resource in GCP.

{rbtgo_trigger_build}.

[[rbtgr_vessel_repo]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_repo_link⟧
{rbtgr_vessel_repo}::
The per-vessel mini-repo on GitHub containing only the build material (cloudbuild.yaml, Dockerfile, build context).
Cloud Build sees only this repo via Developer Connect — the main repo is never exposed.
Managed by
{rbtgo_rubric_inscribe}.

[[rbtgr_provenance]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_slsa_provenance⟧
{rbtgr_provenance}::
SLSA v1.0 build provenance attestation auto-generated by Google for trigger-invoked builds.
Records build inputs, environment, and outputs as a cryptographically signed attestation on the built image.

// ⟦axl_voices axig_developer_connect⟧

The Developer Connect connection providing an OAuth-authenticated bridge from GCP to a GitHub organization.

{rbtgo_gdc_establish}.

[[rbtgi_source_link]]
// ⟦axl_voices axig_repo_link⟧
{rbtgi_source_link}::
A git repository link within the
{rbtgi_source_connection},
binding it to a specific
{rbtgr_vessel_repo}.
Per-vessel, created programmatically by
{rbtgo_rubric_inscribe}.
